phase.name: Phase 1: bin/griot CLI + obvious wrapper kills

contract.goal: Port the largest griot script (capture.ts, ~400 lines source) to a verb handler under .claude/cli/verbs/griot/capture.ts. Both modes preserved: --from-checkin=<path> and --evaluator-finding=<classification>. Byte-equivalent output for --from-checkin mode; --evaluator-finding mode is identical except for a deliberate `capture.ts` → `capture` invocation-reference swap in two prose lines (wrong.md + learning.md provenance footers). D3 of 4 in Phase 1.
acceptance_criteria: captureVerb(rest, ctx) exported from .claude/cli/verbs/griot/capture.ts; parseArgs over rest; ctx.cwd resolves both sessionNotesRoot (learnings/session-notes) and relative checkin paths.
--from-checkin mode writes the same 5 files (prompt/wrong/correction/full_transcript/learning.md) with byte-equivalent content to the legacy script.

--evaluator-finding mode (recurring | generator-antipattern) writes the same 5 files with classification frontmatter; content matches legacy except for the `capture` (was `capture.ts`) invocation-reference swap in wrong.md and learning.md provenance footers.

All legacy error paths preserved: mutual exclusion of --from-checkin / --evaluator-finding; not-yet-supported classifications (catalog-gap, evaluator-conflict, sanctioned-exception); missing required args; folder-collision exit; ambiguous correction; missing checkin file; no-corrections checkin.
Registered in GRIOT_VERBS; help-text entry in VERBS registry.
Sibling test capture.test.ts: 26 tests adapted from legacy capture.test.ts, all passing.
rules_applied: Class-throw-then-catch pattern for cross-function flow (CaptureError; matches D2's ValidationError pattern).
L-013: ground prose claims in execution evidence — byte-parity verified via diff against legacy script before writing the contract claim, not inferred from source-reading.
disqualifiers: Modify or delete legacy .claude/scripts/griot/capture.ts or capture.test.ts (D4's scope).
Change file-content shape beyond the documented capture.ts → capture invocation-reference swap.
Diverge from bin/draft's flat-verb dispatcher architecture.

actions: Wrote .claude/cli/verbs/griot/capture.ts (~400 lines, ports all legacy helpers + both mode handlers; fail() returns DispatchResult; CaptureError class replaces process.exit-from-validator pattern; --evaluator-finding invocation references updated to `capture` from `capture.ts`).
Updated .claude/cli/verbs/griot/index.ts to register capture in GRIOT_VERBS.
Updated .claude/cli/griot.ts VERBS map with help text for capture.

Live byte-parity check via /tmp/capture-parity-test/ fixture: diff of all 5 written files (prompt/wrong/correction/full_transcript/learning) between bin/griot capture --from-checkin and node .claude/scripts/griot/capture.ts --from-checkin → all empty.
Wrote capture.test.ts (389 lines, 26 tests adapted from legacy capture.test.ts to call captureVerb directly with controlled GriotCliContext + tmpdir-cwd fixture).
Ran npm test: 38 files / 525 tests pass (was 37/499 after D2; +1 file +26 tests).
Invoked /guild-validate with evaluator-contract-fit + evaluator-naming; both VERDICT: approved; aggregated approved.
corrections: Initial artifact summary line-number pointers in the evaluator packet were ~20-25 lines off (cited 379-396 / 426 when actual is 401 / 428). Noted by contract-fit evaluator as soft summary-drift, not artifact drift — no rework required. Worth tightening in subsequent packets.

scope: Port capture verb with both modes preserved.
changes_since_previous: Builds on D1 + D2 (checkins 01 + 02). Adds the capture verb; dispatcher registry extended. Legacy scripts still preserved.
notes_for_pr: D3 of 4 in Phase 1. PR refresh deferred until phase close (after D4).

Byte-for-byte parity verified for --from-checkin mode via diff against the legacy script across all 5 output files. --evaluator-finding mode is identical except for two prose lines (wrong.md + learning.md provenance) that intentionally update the invocation reference from `capture.ts` to `capture` to reflect the new CLI surface; that's a deliberate change documented in the contract.
CaptureError class replaces the legacy fail-then-process.exit pattern inside selectCorrection (which was thrown-and-rethrown anyway). Same caller-facing surface (stderr line + exit 1).

captureFromEvaluatorFinding signature changed slightly: now takes the resolved sessionNotesRoot as a parameter (vs computing it from a module-level constant in legacy). The legacy module-level constant pinned SESSION_NOTES_ROOT to process.cwd() at module-load time, which was already a testability issue (tests use execFileSync to chdir into a tmpdir). The new shape passes ctx.cwd explicitly per call, which composes cleanly with the verb-handler signature.

Self-reflection (L-013): packet line-number pointers were off by ~20 lines because I cited approximate positions from memory rather than re-reading the file. Contract-fit evaluator caught it as a soft note. Future packets: cite line ranges only after a fresh Read.
